kw.name: login
kw.doc: to tnter the jupyterlab

kw.name: Open Browser
kw.doc: Opens a new browser instance to the optional ``url``.

kw.name: Maximize Browser Window
kw.doc: Maximizes current browser window.

kw.name: Wait Until Element Is Visible
kw.doc: Waits until the element ``locator`` is visible.

kw.name: Input Text
kw.doc: Types the given ``text`` into the text field identified by ``locator``.

kw.name: Click Button
kw.doc: Clicks the button identified by ``locator``.

kw.name: Wait Until Element Is Visible
kw.doc: Waits until the element ``locator`` is visible.

kw.name: Scroll Element Into View
kw.doc: Scrolls the element identified by ``locator`` into view.

kw.name: Wait Until Element Is Visible
kw.doc: Waits until the element ``locator`` is visible.

kw.name: Click Element
kw.doc: Click the element identified by ``locator``.
kw.msg: Clicking element 'xpath://div[@class='jp-LauncherCard' and @title='Create a new runtime template']'.

kw.name: Wait Until Element Is Visible
kw.doc: Waits until the element ``locator`` is visible.

kw.name: Click Element
kw.doc: Click the element identified by ``locator``.

kw.name: Page Should Contain
kw.doc: Verifies that current page contains ``text``.
kw.msg: Current page contains text 'Dataproc Serverless Notebooks'.

kw.doc: Returns the given values which can then be assigned to a variables.

kw.name: Get Element Count
kw.doc: Returns the number of elements matching ``locator``.
kw.msg: Keyword 'Get Element Count' found both from a custom library 'Selenium2Library' and a standard library 'XML'. The custom keyword is used. To select explicitly, and to get rid of this warning, use either 'Selenium2Library.Get Element Count' or 'XML.Get Element Count'.

kw.doc: Returns the given values which can then be assigned to a variables.

kw.msg: Typing text 'prop_val2' into text field 'xpath://span[text()='ADD PROPERTY']/parent::button/parent::div//label[text()='Value 2']/parent::div//input[@class='MuiInputBase-input MuiOutlinedInput-input MuiInputBase-inputSizeSmall css-k9lw1l']'.

kw.name: Scroll Element Into View
kw.doc: Scrolls the element identified by ``locator`` into view.

kw.name: Click Element
kw.doc: Click the element identified by ``locator``.

kw.name: Wait Until Element Is Visible
kw.doc: Waits until the element ``locator`` is visible.

kw.name: Click Element
kw.doc: Click the element identified by ``locator``.

kw.doc: Types the given ``text`` into the text field identified by ``locator``.

kw.name: Scroll Element Into View
kw.doc: Scrolls the element identified by ``locator`` into view.

kw.name: Click Element
kw.doc: Click the element identified by ``locator``.

kw.name: open_config_setup_page
kw.doc: opening config setup page from launcher

kw.name: Wait Until Element Is Visible
kw.doc: Waits until the element ``locator`` is visible.

kw.name: Click Element
kw.doc: Click the element identified by ``locator``.

kw.name: Wait Until Element Is Visible
kw.doc: Waits until the element ``locator`` is visible.

kw.name: Click Element
kw.doc: Click the element identified by ``locator``.

kw.name: Wait Until Element Is Visible
kw.doc: Waits until the element ``locator`` is visible.

kw.name: Wait Until Page Contains
kw.doc: Waits until ``text`` appears on the current page.

kw.name: logout
kw.doc: closing the Browser

kw.name: Close Browser
kw.doc: Closes the current browser.

errors.msg: Keyword 'Get Element Count' found both from a custom library 'Selenium2Library' and a standard library 'XML'. The custom keyword is used. To select explicitly, and to get rid of this warning, use either 'Selenium2Library.Get Element Count' or 'XML.Get Element Count'.

Keyword 'Get Element Count' found both from a custom library 'Selenium2Library' and a standard library 'XML'. The custom keyword is used. To select explicitly, and to get rid of this warning, use either 'Selenium2Library.Get Element Count' or 'XML.Get Element Count'.
